title: d: non-blocking MCP server startup — defer reconcile to background + 5s gate at handler entry
scope: packages/server/src/index.ts startStdioServer + tool handler entry shim
description: Current MCP startStdioServer awaits reconcileKnowledge before responding to client handshake — slow KB reconciles delay first-call latency by 2-15s. Refactor: (1) complete server.connect handshake immediately; (2) kick reconcile off as a background Promise stored on a context; (3) each tool handler gates at entry on awaitWithTimeout(reconcilePromise, 5000ms) — if still pending after 5s, proceed with stale meta and emit a fail-loud `reconcile_pending` warning in the tool response.

files.change: Split: connect handshake first, store reconcilePromise on serverContext, do not await before listen
Add awaitWithTimeout(serverContext.reconcilePromise, 5000) — on timeout, attach `reconcile_pending: true` to response.meta + emit warning event
Add reconcile_failed + meta_stale_at_handler event types for fail-loud telemetry

implementation: Read services/plan-context.ts auto-heal path (TASK-005) to align with the new reconcilePromise convention
Implement startStdioServer refactor: kick reconcile as `void reconcile().catch(...)` stored on context
Wrap each tool handler entry with the gate; on timeout, log + still serve the call from stale meta
Add unit tests: (a) handshake responds <100ms even when reconcile fakes 2s delay, (b) handler within 5s gets fresh meta, (c) handler after 6s gets stale meta + reconcile_pending warning
Coordinate with TASK-005 — both touch reconcile flow

reference.pattern: Existing tool-handler entry shim (if any) — else introduce a small wrap helper

criteria: MCP handshake completes within 100ms regardless of KB size
Tool handlers wait at most 5s for reconcile, then proceed with stale meta + warning
reconcile_failed + meta_stale_at_handler events appear in events.jsonl on simulated slow-reconcile run
Unit tests pass for three timing scenarios

rationale.chosen_approach: Non-blocking startup + per-handler timeout gate — best UX (fast handshake) + correctness (fail-loud on slow reconcile)
alternatives_considered: Keep blocking startup (rejected: scaling failure on large KBs)
Skip gate, always serve stale until reconcile finishes (rejected: silent staleness violates feedback_cli_design fail-loud)
rationale.tradeoffs: Slightly more complex server lifecycle — manageable; well-tested timing scenarios

manual_checks: Start MCP server with KB containing 100+ entries; measure first plan_context call latency (target <100ms handshake)
success_metrics: MCP handshake latency p95 < 100ms after rc.23
